agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering a premium, time-sensitive courier service with a sub-2-hour delivery window, a fixed surcharge, and a refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery notes across PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a subdomain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-window commitments, driver eligibility constraints, and a distinct refund policy separate from standard delivery.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, subject to eligibility constraints." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A fixed surcharge of $12.99 applied to orders that select the Express Delivery option." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged on all orders, independent of the express surcharge. Never refunded under the express refund policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour time span within which an express delivery is expected to be completed. Start point is currently ambiguous (see escalations)." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility Zone", "definition": "A radius of 10km from a warehouse within which a customer's delivery address must fall to qualify for express delivery." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute window after order placement within which a driver must physically pick up the express order from the warehouse." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "The condition where an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering the refund policy." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "When an assigned driver abandons an express delivery mid-fulfillment, requiring reassignment." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancellation within which a replacement driver must be assigned. Failure triggers an automatic refund." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "Legal's characterization of the express fee refund — a voluntary gesture, not a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion: if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, no express fee refund is issued regardless of whether the window was missed." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select Express Delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of at least one warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical/logistical constraint. Offering express outside this radius makes the 2-hour window physically impossible and would create systematic refund liability.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: An order with express selected WHERE distance(delivery_address, nearest_warehouse) > 10km is REJECTED at checkout."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the Base Delivery Fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by product/finance leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: An express order's total delivery charges = base_delivery_fee + $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operations states the 2-hour window is unachievable if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a physical time constraint that protects the integrity of the delivery window.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: If no driver picks up an express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system escalates (behavior TBD — see escalations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating >= 4.0 stars are eligible to be assigned express orders.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The rating threshold is an operational quality policy. Operations leadership could adjust this threshold based on driver supply.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: The system NEVER assigns an express order to a driver with rating < 4.0."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an express delivery misses the 2-hour window, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund scope is a business/financial policy. Legal explicitly classifies this as a goodwill gesture, not a contractual obligation.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: A missed-window refund record is created for exactly the express fee amount, not the base fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, regardless of whether the delivery window was missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund exclusion policy that could be adjusted by Finance or Customer Success.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: A missed-window event WHERE address_verified = false results in NO refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, an automatic refund of the express fee is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute threshold and auto-refund behavior are operational policies that could be tuned.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: A driver_cancellation event with no replacement_assigned event within 10 minutes triggers an automatic express_fee_refund event."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers receive real-time tracking of their assigned driver.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Real-time tracking is a product feature/capability, not a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: An active express delivery exposes a real-time driver location stream to the customer."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is a finance operations policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "ASSERT: A refund record created for a missed window has a processing_deadline <= order_date + 3 business days."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10.0km from nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "Boundary condition: must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid customer frustration at boundary." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers (4-star+) are available at time of order", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Should express option be hidden/disabled? Or should the order be accepted and queued? Risk of immediate pickup SLA breach." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA (15 min) is breached but delivery still completes within 2 hours", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Is this a success or a process violation? Operations treats pickup SLA as critical, but outcome was met." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA is breached and delivery also misses the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Should there be an internal escalation path distinct from a normal missed window?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 110 of a 120-minute window, leaving only 10 minutes for replacement AND completion", "expected_behavior": "Replacement within 10 minutes is nearly impossible to also deliver. The 10-minute replacement window and 2-hour delivery window interact in a way that guarantees a missed window. Should auto-refund trigger immediately?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to a location >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Should express be auto-downgraded? Should the change be rejected?" },
    { "scenario": "'Wrong address' determination is disputed by customer", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Who adjudicates? What evidence is required? This is a refund-blocking determination with no defined process." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple refund triggers fire simultaneously (missed window + failed replacement)", "expected_behavior": "Must ensure only one express fee refund is issued, not two. Idempotency of refund is critical." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:55 PM — does the 2-hour window span midnight or business day boundaries?", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Are express deliveries available 24/7? If not, what are the operating hours?" },
    { "scenario": "Warehouse has no inventory for the ordered items at pickup time", "expected_behavior": "Undefined. Express eligibility assumes item availability. Stock-out after order placement breaks the entire flow." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee of $12.99 per order represents incremental revenue. However, refund liability is significant: every missed window, every failed driver replacement costs $12.99 in goodwill refunds. The wrong-address exclusion is the only refund gate. Without tight operational controls on pickup SLA and driver availability, refund rates could erode the entire express margin. Finance must model expected miss rates to validate unit economics.",
    "operational": "Introduces hard real-time constraints into fulfillment: 15-min pickup SLA, 10-min replacement window, 2-hour delivery window. This requires dedicated express driver pools, real-time dispatch optimization, driver rating monitoring, and geofencing infrastructure. Operations burden is substantial — this is not just a fee toggle on standard delivery.",
    "user": "High customer expectations set by 'guarantee' language (PM, Customer Success) but legally characterized as 'best effort' (Legal). This mismatch is a customer trust time bomb. Real-time tracking is a strong value-add. The refund policy provides a safety net, but the wrong-address exclusion could generate disputes and negative sentiment if not handled transparently.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: The fundamental disagreement between stakeholders on whether this is a 'guarantee' or 'best effort' creates legal exposure if marketing uses guarantee language that Legal has rejected. SECONDARY RISK: Refund automation (driver cancellation) vs manual processing (3 business days) creates inconsistent customer experiences. TERTIARY RISK: No defined behavior for pickup SLA breach creates a silent failure mode where orders are technically active but operationally doomed.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Expect a guaranteed 2-hour delivery based on UI language; may receive best-effort service. Real-time tracking adds value. Wrong-address disputes could damage trust." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "New eligibility constraint (4-star minimum) creates a two-tier system. Express drivers face tighter time pressure. Rating becomes higher-stakes." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup SLA — orders must be pre-staged or prioritized. Express orders disrupt FIFO fulfillment of standard orders." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Must process refunds within 3 business days. Must track and report on express fee revenue vs refund liability. Unit economics depend on miss rate." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle wrong-address disputes, missed window complaints, and confusion about guarantee vs best-effort. Needs clear escalation paths and decision authority." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no contractual guarantee is implied. Refund framed as goodwill, not obligation." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is very low due to a fundamental contradiction between stakeholders on the core value proposition (guarantee vs best-effort). This is not a minor policy disagreement — it determines the legal framing, marketing language, refund obligation model, and customer expectations for the entire product. Additionally, multiple critical operational behaviors are undefined (pickup SLA breach, driver unavailability, operating hours). Acceptance criteria cannot be written until the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is resolved and key gaps are filled.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is agreed upon across stakeholders",
      "10km eligibility radius is clearly stated",
      "4-star driver rating threshold is clearly stated",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded under express policy",
      "Wrong-address exclusion on refunds is clearly stated by Finance"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — stakeholders directly contradict",
      "When the 2-hour window starts (order placement vs pickup vs dispatch)",
      "What happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached",
      "Whether auto-refund (driver cancellation) and manual refund (missed window) follow different processing paths",
      "Operating hours and availability constraints for express service",
      "How 'wrong address' is determined and disputed"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km radius is measured as straight-line distance, not driving distance — this is not specified",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement — PM says 'within 2 hours' without specifying start point",
      "Driver rating is a rolling average, not a per-delivery rating — not specified",
      "Express eligibility is evaluated at checkout time, not continuously during delivery",
      "Only one express fee refund can be issued per order regardless of how many triggers fire"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery window as a 'guarantee' shown prominently to customers. Legal explicitly states this CANNOT be a guarantee — it must be framed as 'best effort' with refunds as a 'goodwill gesture.' This is a foundational contradiction that affects every customer-facing surface, marketing material, refund policy framing, and potential legal liability. The product cannot ship until this is resolved.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Legal", "Customer Success", "Marketing", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as 'best effort' per Legal — use language like 'Express target: 2 hours' with refund as goodwill. Lower customer expectations but legally safe.",
        "B: Frame as guarantee with Legal carve-outs — Terms of Service define conditions under which the guarantee is void (wrong address, force majeure, etc). Higher customer expectations, higher refund liability, requires Legal sign-off on specific T&C language.",
        "C: Hybrid — use 'Express Promise' language that implies commitment without contractual guarantee. Customer-facing is aspirational, T&C are protective."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C (Hybrid) — but Legal MUST draft and approve the specific language. This cannot be resolved by engineering or product alone. BLOCKER: No acceptance criteria can be finalized until this is resolved."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refund should be 'automatic' when driver replacement fails within 10 minutes. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These describe fundamentally different refund mechanisms: one is event-driven and instant, the other is batch-processed with a 3-day SLA. Are there two different refund paths? Or is the 'automatic' refund also subject to the 3-day processing window?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Success", "Engineering", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: All express refunds are automatic (instant credit) — Finance's 3-day window applies to back-office reconciliation, not customer-facing timing.",
        "B: All express refunds are processed within 3 business days — Customer Success's 'automatic' means 'no manual approval needed' but not 'instant credit.'",
        "C: Two refund tiers — driver cancellation refunds are instant; missed-window refunds are processed within 3 days."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B is most likely what was meant — 'automatic' refers to no human approval required, not instant processing. But Finance and Customer Success must align explicitly."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder defined what happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached. This is an operational invariant (Operations says 2-hour window is unachievable without it) but there is no defined system behavior: Does the order get cancelled? Downgraded to standard? Does the customer get notified? Is a refund triggered? This is a critical failure mode with no defined response.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Engineering", "Customers", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-cancel the express upgrade and notify customer, refund express fee immediately.",
        "B: Escalate to dispatch manager for manual intervention, keep express status.",
        "C: Auto-reassign to another express-eligible driver, restart the 15-minute pickup clock.",
        "D: Downgrade to standard delivery, refund express fee, notify customer."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option D — downgrade to standard with proactive refund and customer notification. This preserves trust and avoids a guaranteed missed window."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The start time of the 2-hour delivery window is never explicitly defined. PM says 'within 2 hours' — but from when? Order placement? Payment confirmation? Driver pickup? This ambiguity of even a few minutes compounds with the 15-minute pickup SLA, effectively leaving only ~1h45m for transit.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Engineering", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Window starts at order placement (customer's perspective — clearest, most aggressive).",
        "B: Window starts at payment confirmation (accounts for payment processing delays).",
        "C: Window starts at driver pickup (operational perspective — most generous for operations, least transparent to customer)."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — from the customer's perspective, the clock starts when they place the order. This is the only interpretation that matches customer expectations. Operations must plan around this."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 10km eligibility radius is not specified as straight-line (geodesic) distance or driving/routing distance. In urban areas with rivers, highways, or one-way systems, these can differ by 2-3x. A customer 8km straight-line but 18km driving distance would qualify for express but be physically unreachable in 2 hours.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Straight-line (geodesic) distance — simpler to compute, but can admit unreachable addresses.",
        "B: Driving distance via routing engine — more accurate but adds API dependency and latency at checkout.",
        "C: Straight-line with a tighter radius (e.g., 7km) as a conservative proxy for driving distance."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — driving distance is the only measurement that reflects physical reality. The 10km radius was presumably conceived in terms of reachability, not geometry."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours are defined for express delivery. Is it available 24/7? During warehouse operating hours only? What about weekends, holidays, or periods of low driver supply? A 2am express order has very different fulfillment characteristics than a 2pm order.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Limit to warehouse operating hours only.",
        "B: 24/7 availability with dynamic express-eligible checks (only offered when drivers and warehouse are active).",
        "C: Fixed published hours (e.g., 8am-10pm) regardless of warehouse schedule."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — express should only be offered when it can actually be fulfilled. Dynamic availability prevents promising what cannot be delivered."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define acceptance criteria when stakeholders fundamentally disagree on whether the core value proposition is a 'guarantee' or 'best effort'.", "business_rule_broken": "The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction (PM/CS vs Legal) makes it impossible to define correct system behavior for customer-facing messaging, refund obligation triggers, and SLA monitoring." },
    { "violation": "Pickup SLA breach has no defined system behavior.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-3 defines the 15-minute pickup constraint but no stakeholder defined the failure mode. Without this, the system has a critical undefined state transition." }
  ]
}
